page: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, delivering 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance bundles eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with a combined VRAM of 179 GB. Local storage consists of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks reaches top-tier status, while uncached large-block read and write speeds remain strong. The instance excels in parallelized tasks, achieving top-tier results in Passmark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and Geekbench ray tracing. Notably, LLM inference benchmarks for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models consistently rank in the top tier.

The hardware profile of the g5.48xlarge represents a high-density resource allocation, offering a qualitative balance of massive parallel compute, high GPU memory capacity, and fast local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core efficiency, making it less suitable for single-threaded tasks. However, its high multi-core density and GPU acceleration make it highly cost-efficient for large-scale deployments that can fully utilize its parallel architecture. This instance is optimized for demanding workloads such as large language model inference, ray tracing, high-throughput database operations, and multi-threaded scientific computing.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a high-density, GPU-accelerated server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Acceleration is driven by eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-threaded operations, Redis database transactions, and large language model inference across various model sizes, contrasting with lower single-core performance. With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, this instance is highly suited for parallelized workloads, including LLM prompt processing, ray tracing, and intensive database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, eight NVIDIA A10G GPUs, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 8 NVIDIA A10G GPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for parallel and LLM workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring eight accelerators, massive local NVMe storage, and high-bandwidth networking for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 179 GB total VRAM
192 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR4 memory
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and multi-threaded benchmarks
Optimized for parallel computing, ray tracing, and database operations
